title: Catalyst Project Config
description: Schema for .catalyst/config.json — the Layer-1 project config committed to the repository. Contains team-wide settings safe to share. Secrets and host-specific overrides belong in the Layer-2 machine config (~/.config/catalyst/config.json).

catalyst.description: Root namespace for all Catalyst configuration.

projectKey.description: Short identifier for this project, used as a namespace for machine-config lookup (~/.config/catalyst/config-{projectKey}.json) and as a display label.

ticketPrefix.description: Linear ticket prefix used when constructing ticket IDs (e.g. 'CTL' produces 'CTL-123').

teamKey.description: Linear team key string (e.g. 'CTL'). Required for Linear integration to function.

stateMap.description: Maps canonical Catalyst phase names to Linear workflow state name strings. All values must exactly match the state names configured in your Linear team.

backlog.description: Linear state name for backlog items.

todo.description: Linear state name for to-do / ready items.

triage.description: Linear state name while in the triage phase.

research.description: Linear state name while in the research phase.

planning.description: Linear state name while in the plan phase.

inProgress.description: Linear state name while in the implement phase.

verifying.description: Linear state name while in the verify phase.

reviewing.description: Linear state name while in the review phase.

remediating.description: Linear state name while in the remediate phase.

inReview.description: Linear state name after a PR is open (monitor-merge phase).

done.description: Linear state name for completed tickets.

canceled.description: Linear state name for canceled tickets.

stateIds.description: Optional cache of Linear state name → UUID mappings. Populated automatically by setup scripts. Allows the daemon to avoid repeated API lookups.

teamId.description: UUID of the Linear team. Populated by setup scripts. Used to scope API calls.

profile.description: HumanLayer profile name for the thoughts CLI. null disables thoughts persistence.

directory.description: Absolute or repo-relative path to the thoughts directory where agent notes are written.

user.description: Username passed to the thoughts CLI. null uses the CLI default.

suppressVersionWarning.description: When true, suppresses the monitor startup banner warning about version mismatches between the installed CLI and the running daemon.

repoColors.description: Maps repository full names (owner/repo) to CSS color strings for color-coding in the monitor dashboard.

teams.description: List of Linear teams to display in the monitor, each linked to a VCS repository.

vcsRepo.description: GitHub repository full name (owner/repo) associated with this team.

botUserId.description: Linear user UUID of the Catalyst app-actor (the 'Linear for Agents' app user, installed via CTL-550). Used by orch-monitor and the execution-core daemon to suppress bot self-echo comments/updates and bot-authored issue events, preventing write loops (CTL-749). Required for the Linear app-actor comms channel — i.e. when the execution-core daemon mirrors phase-agent output to Linear and wakes on human replies (CTL-550 / CTL-549 / CTL-749); omitting it makes the system treat the agent's own comments as human input. Obtain it from the app-actor access token's viewer.id (NOT a personal/admin token): query{viewer{id}}. This value is workspace-specific — set it only in the local .catalyst/config.json, not in committed templates (use null + a placeholder there). Default null disables suppression.

orchestration.description: Orchestration engine settings controlling how Catalyst dispatches and manages agent work.

dispatchMode.description: Selects the orchestration strategy. 'phase-agents' runs one short-lived claude --bg job per pipeline phase (triage→research→plan→implement→verify→review→pr→monitor-merge→monitor-deploy). 'oneshot-legacy' runs a single long-lived claude -p /catalyst-legacy:oneshot job per ticket. Default is 'phase-agents'.

executionCore.description: Concurrency settings for the execution-core daemon. These are seed values; the Layer-2 machine config wins per-field when present.

maxParallel.description: Desired maximum number of concurrently running phase-agent bg jobs. The scheduler will not dispatch new work when the running count reaches this value. Overrideable per-machine in Layer-2 config.

minParallel.description: Minimum concurrency floor; the scheduler always tries to keep at least this many jobs active when eligible work exists.

maxParallelCeiling.description: Hard upper cap on maxParallel. Dynamic concurrency scaling will never exceed this value regardless of Layer-2 overrides.

eligibleQuery.description: DEPRECATED. The daemon now reads eligibleQuery from ~/catalyst/execution-core/registry.json per-project. This field is ignored by the execution-core scheduler.
eligibleQuery.$comment: DEPRECATED: moved to ~/catalyst/execution-core/registry.json. The daemon's scheduler reads eligibleQuery from the registry, not from this config field. This object is retained for backward compatibility with older CLI versions that may still write it.

status.description: Linear workflow state name to filter eligible tickets (e.g. 'Todo').

team.description: Linear team key to filter eligible tickets. null means all enrolled teams.

project.description: Linear project name to filter eligible tickets. null means no project filter.

label.description: Linear label to filter eligible tickets. null means no label filter.

priority.description: Linear priority (1=urgent, 2=high, 3=medium, 4=low) to filter eligible tickets. null means no priority filter.

phaseAgents.description: Per-phase agent configuration for the phase-agents dispatch mode.

models.description: Maps phase names to Claude model strings. Allows routing expensive phases (e.g. implement) to a more capable model while using faster models for lighter phases. Layer-2 machine config wins over this field when present.

turnCaps.description: Maps phase names to maximum turn counts. A phase that reaches its turn cap emits status 'turn-cap-exhausted' and stops. The daemon may revive it or escalate to needs-human.

orphanReaper.description: Settings for the orphan reaper, which detects and cleans up bg jobs that have exited without emitting a terminal signal status.

enabled.description: When false, the orphan reaper is disabled. Useful for debugging to prevent the daemon from terminating stalled workers.

intervalSeconds.description: How often (in seconds) the orphan reaper sweep runs.

minIdleSeconds.description: Minimum number of seconds a worker's signal file must be unmodified before the reaper considers it a candidate for reclaim.

feedback.description: Automatic feedback filing settings. When enabled, agents can file issues to a GitHub repository when they encounter unexpected behavior.

autoFile.description: When true, agents automatically file GitHub issues for feedback events without prompting.

githubRepo.description: GitHub repository full name (owner/repo) where feedback issues are filed.

labels.description: List of label strings applied to auto-filed feedback issues.

filter.description: Catalyst filter / broker settings. The filter is not a separate daemon; it is part of the broker process.

groqModel.description: Groq model identifier used by the broker's LLM-based event filtering. Requires a GROQ_API_KEY in the environment.

deploy.description: Repository-specific deploy configuration consumed by phase-monitor-deploy. The shape is repo-specific; additionalProperties are allowed.
